title: UNIX Process Environment List Structure
description: A visual representation of how the environment list is stored and accessed by a process, showing the relationship between the process's memory, the `environ` pointer, and individual environment variables.
relationships: The 'environ' pointer resides in the process's memory and points to an array of character pointers
Each element in the array points to a string containing a single environment variable in the format NAME=VALUE
The array of pointers is terminated by a NULL pointer
